title: centaurea farsistanica
intro: Centaurea farsistanica, also known as the Iranian knapweed, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family native to Iran. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of soothing and protecting the body. Centaurea farsistanica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for internal use, or applied topically as an ointment or cream for external application.
